questions.q: What is the main purpose of federalism?
opts: (A) To provide a single, unified government
(B) To divide power between a central and regional governments
(C) To abolish regional governments
(D) To increase the power of the central government
questions.explanation: Federalism divides power between a central government and regional governments to ensure representation and decision-making at different levels.

questions.q: Who proposed the concept of federalism?
opts: (A) James Madison
(B) Alexander Hamilton
(C) Benjamin Franklin
(D) George Washington
questions.explanation: James Madison is often credited with proposing the concept of federalism, which he discussed in The Federalist Papers.

questions.q: What is the main advantage of federalism?
opts: (A) Increased centralization of power
(B) Decentralization of power and representation
(C) Single, unified decision-making
(D) Increased regional autonomy
questions.explanation: The main advantage of federalism is the decentralization of power and representation, which allows for more diverse and inclusive decision-making.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a federal system of government?
opts: (A) Unitary system
(B) Confederal system
(C) Parliamentary system
(D) Presidential system
questions.explanation: A confederal system, where power is divided between a central government and regional governments, is an example of a federal system of government.

questions.q: What is the difference between a confederation and a federation?
opts: (A) A confederation has a stronger central government, while a federation has a weaker central government
(B) A confederation has a weaker central government, while a federation has a stronger central government
(C) A confederation has no central government, while a federation has a strong central government
(D) A confederation has a strong central government, while a federation has no central government
questions.explanation: A confederation has a weaker central government, while a federation has a stronger central government, which has more powers and authority.

questions.q: What is the main weakness of federalism?
opts: (A) Lack of centralization of power
(B) Increase in regional autonomy
(C) Difficulty in decision-making
(D) Increased regional conflicts
questions.explanation: The main weakness of federalism is the difficulty in decision-making, as power is divided between different levels of government.

questions.q: How does federalism promote representation?
opts: (A) By allowing only the central government to represent regional interests
(B) By dividing power and representation between different levels of government
(C) By abolishing regional governments and giving more power to the central government
(D) By increasing the power of regional governments at the expense of the central government
questions.explanation: Federalism promotes representation by dividing power and representation between different levels of government, allowing for more diverse and inclusive decision-making.

questions.q: What is the role of the central government in a federal system?
opts: (A) To make all decisions and have complete control over regional governments
(B) To provide a framework for decision-making and regulate regional governments
(C) To have no role and regional governments are completely autonomous
(D) To make decisions only in areas of national interest
questions.explanation: In a federal system, the central government provides a framework for decision-making and regulates regional governments, while also having some powers in areas of national interest.

questions.q: How does federalism affect regional autonomy?
opts: (A) It reduces regional autonomy and increases centralization of power
(B) It increases regional autonomy and reduces centralization of power
(C) It abolishes regional governments and gives more power to the central government
(D) It has no effect on regional autonomy
questions.explanation: Federalism increases regional autonomy by dividing power and representation between different levels of government, allowing regional governments to make decisions in areas of regional interest.

questions.q: What is the relationship between federalism and democracy?
opts: (A) Federalism is incompatible with democracy, as it reduces regional representation
(B) Federalism promotes democracy by increasing regional representation
(C) Federalism has no relationship with democracy
(D) Federalism reduces regional representation and increases centralization of power
questions.explanation: Federalism promotes democracy by increasing regional representation and allowing for more diverse and inclusive decision-making.

questions.q: How does federalism affect national unity?
opts: (A) It reduces national unity and increases regional divisions
(B) It increases national unity and reduces regional divisions
(C) It abolishes regional governments and gives more power to the central government
(D) It has no effect on national unity
questions.explanation: Federalism increases national unity by providing a framework for decision-making and regulating regional governments, while also allowing for regional representation and autonomy.

questions.q: What is the role of the judiciary in a federal system?
opts: (A) To make laws and regulate regional governments
(B) To interpret laws and ensure their consistency with the constitution
(C) To have no role and regional governments are completely autonomous
(D) To make decisions only in areas of national interest
questions.explanation: In a federal system, the judiciary interprets laws and ensures their consistency with the constitution, providing a check on the power of regional governments and the central government.

questions.q: How does federalism affect economic development?
opts: (A) It reduces economic development and increases regional poverty
(B) It increases economic development and reduces regional poverty
(C) It abolishes regional governments and gives more power to the central government
(D) It has no effect on economic development
questions.explanation: Federalism increases economic development by allowing regional governments to make decisions in areas of regional interest, such as infrastructure development and taxation.

questions.q: What is the relationship between federalism and regionalism?
opts: (A) Federalism reduces regionalism and increases centralization of power
(B) Federalism promotes regionalism by increasing regional autonomy
(C) Federalism has no relationship with regionalism
(D) Federalism abolishes regional governments and gives more power to the central government
questions.explanation: Federalism promotes regionalism by increasing regional autonomy and allowing regional governments to make decisions in areas of regional interest.

questions.q: How does federalism affect social justice?
opts: (A) It reduces social justice and increases regional inequality
(B) It increases social justice and reduces regional inequality
(C) It abolishes regional governments and gives more power to the central government
(D) It has no effect on social justice
questions.explanation: Federalism increases social justice by allowing regional governments to make decisions in areas of social interest, such as education and healthcare.

questions.q: What is the role of the central government in regulating regional governments?
opts: (A) To make laws and regulate regional governments without their consent
(B) To negotiate and regulate regional governments through agreements and treaties
(C) To have no role and regional governments are completely autonomous
(D) To make decisions only in areas of national interest
questions.explanation: In a federal system, the central government regulates regional governments through agreements and treaties, providing a framework for decision-making and ensuring consistency with the constitution.

questions.q: How does federalism affect the relationship between the central government and regional governments?
opts: (A) It reduces the relationship and increases regional autonomy
(B) It increases the relationship and reduces regional autonomy
(C) It abolishes regional governments and gives more power to the central government
(D) It has no effect on the relationship
questions.topic: Relationship between Central and Regional Governments
questions.explanation: Federalism increases the relationship between the central government and regional governments by providing a framework for decision-making and regulating regional governments, while also allowing for regional representation and autonomy.

questions.q: What is the relationship between federalism and global governance?
opts: (A) Federalism reduces global governance and increases regional autonomy
(B) Federalism promotes global governance by increasing regional representation
(C) Federalism has no relationship with global governance
(D) Federalism abolishes regional governments and gives more power to the central government
questions.explanation: Federalism promotes global governance by increasing regional representation and allowing regional governments to participate in international organizations and agreements.

questions.q: How does federalism affect the distribution of power between the central government and regional governments?
opts: (A) It reduces the power of regional governments and increases centralization of power
(B) It increases the power of regional governments and reduces centralization of power
(C) It abolishes regional governments and gives more power to the central government
(D) It has no effect on the distribution of power
questions.explanation: Federalism increases the power of regional governments by dividing power and representation between different levels of government, while also allowing for regional autonomy and decision-making.

questions.q: What is the role of the constitution in a federal system?
opts: (A) To make laws and regulate regional governments
(B) To provide a framework for decision-making and regulate regional governments
(C) To have no role and regional governments are completely autonomous
(D) To make decisions only in areas of national interest
questions.explanation: In a federal system, the constitution provides a framework for decision-making and regulates regional governments, while also ensuring consistency with the constitution and protecting individual rights.
